description_long: This cowshed features a large, sloped gable roof covered in reddish-brown tiles, providing ample shelter for livestock. The front facade is open with a wide covered porch area, supported by simple posts, and includes a visible entrance and a wooden chair. The walls are constructed from a combination of tan plaster and a lower section of gray stone or concrete blocks, offering both durability and insulation. Windows are present on the side, allowing natural light into the interior, while the rear and one side are mostly solid for protection. The structure is elevated slightly above the ground with a broad, flat base, and the overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function over ornamentation. The cowshed is typical of rural or peri-urban agricultural buildings, blending practical materials and straightforward construction.
description: A large cowshed with a reddish-brown gable roof, tan and gray stone walls, and an open front porch supported by posts. The structure is functional and robust, with windows on the side and a wide sheltered area.
description_view_0: The front view shows the open porch area, main entrance, and a wooden chair under the wide gable roof.
description_view_1: The side view reveals a solid wall with a single window and the continuation of the stone base and tan upper wall.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a fully enclosed wall with no windows or doors, emphasizing the protective function.
description_view_3: The opposite side view shows the solid wall and the depth of the porch area under the extended roof.
attribute_door_style: single wooden plank door, simple construction
attribute_window_presence: two rectangular windows on the side, sliding or fixed style
